calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-08-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lena Sullivan in Propria Persona.

The Court confirmed with Ms. Sullivan that the sale has closed, stated that this is all that was needed, that it will take the accounting petition under submission and that it looked like it will be granted subject to review of the order.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado: Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Continued To Sep-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-14-2015 Continued To Aug-18-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lena Sullivan in Propria Persona.

Ms. Sullivan reported that the sale has not closed, that HUD has been dragging its feet, that she is dealing with squatters and that the 90 days has run out on the appraisal and a new appraisal will take place today. The court stated that it will need an amended petition that reflects the sale so that it can be distributed and accounted for properly and that there are lots of issues that need to be addressed and stated that Ms. Sullivan might want legal assistance. The accounting petition was continued to August 18, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-16-2015 Continued To Jul-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-05-2015 Continued To Jun-16-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-07-2015 Continued To May-05-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-24-2015 Continued To Apr-07-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lena Sullivan in Propria Persona.

Ms. Sullivan reported that amended petition was filed yesterday, the house was sold on a short term sale, she is waiting for HUD approval and needs clarification on the first order in regards to the distribution to the beneficiaries. The court stated that it cannot give legal advice, that she needs to file a petition for final distribution but the court cannot tell her how to draw it up, recommended she speak with an attorney, and clarified if notice had been sent on the amended petition filed yesterday. The accounting petition was continued to April 7, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow time for notice.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Murillo. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-03-2015 Continued To Feb-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Continued To Feb-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lena Sullivan in Propria Persona.

Ms. Sullivan stated they have a buyer for the property, that escrow is open and will be a blocked account, that they are waiting for a demand from HUD and that HUD requested a re-appraisal. Ms. Sullivan also stated that the offer was amended to the appraised value of the property, that they are waiting for HUD's approval, that the property is under water, that she has requested an accounting from HUD for amounts borrowed by her Aunt and that she was supposed to have received the information from HUD last week. The petition for final distribution was continued to February 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Park. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-18-2014 Continued To Jan-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lena Sullivan in Propria Persona.

Ms. Sullivan stated that the property was listed, that they have an offer, that she has the pay off figure for HUD, that HUD will take every dime and that there will be no money left to pay the Beneficiaries. Ms. Sullivan also stated that the amount requested by HUD is mostly for interest and that she has no authority to ask them to reduce it. The Court raised the issue of having someone assist with the sale, assist in dealing with HUD and take a look at the paper work. Ms. Sullivan was directed to speak with the Probate Examiner to discuss how to proceed with close of the Estate. Status on close of the Estate was continued to January 13, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-21-2014 Continued To Nov-18-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-30-2014 Continued To Oct-21-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Advanced From Sep-22-14 To Aug-12-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-12-2014 Continued To Sep-30-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lena Sullivan in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it's concern is that Ms. Sullivan has a life estate in the property, that there are cash bequests and that Ms. Sullivan is asking that the property be distrubuted to her for refinancing to pay the reverse mortgage. Ms. Sullivan explained that the individuals were to receive bank accounts that no longer exist, that the will was written 20-years ago and that the funds from the reverse mortgage were used to provide care for the Aunt. Ms. Sullivan also stated that said funds were exhausted, that she used her own funds to pay for the Aunt's care, that there were no bank accounts at the time of death and that the plan is to move into the property if she is able to pay off HUD. In addition, Ms. Sullivan stated that HUD is on hold, that they could come in at any time, that she could qualify for a hard money loan but she has to have title to refinance and pay HUD, and that she is the Heir. The Court stated that the will can not be ignored, that the concern is the house get sold and the Beneficiaries not get paid, and asked Ms. Sullivan to speak with the Probate Examiner. Upon returning, Ms. Sullivan stated that she realizes that she will have to sell the house on the market and asked for a lien on the property for the Beneficiaries to paid out of escrow. The Court suggested the sales proceeds be placed into a blocked account. The Petition for Final Distribution was continued to September 30, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will issue an interim order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-07-2014 Continued To Sep-22-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
